jokes.text: D'oh! [Screams]
jokes.setup: Opening credits sequence with Homer presumably getting hurt
jokes.punchline: His signature exclamation of pain/frustration
jokes.explanation: Iconic catchphrase perfectly integrated to character. Maximum economy with high recognition value.

jokes.text: Homer, that's dishwashing liquid! Yeah, but what Are ya gonna do?
jokes.setup: Homer about to drink free sample of 'Lemon Time'
jokes.punchline: Homer's resigned acceptance of drinking soap
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer characterization - his lazy fatalism about obvious mistakes. Great economy in the delivery.

jokes.text: Howdy-do, neighbor! Good pipe weather. Thought I'd fire up the briar. Can't talk. Busy. Okilly-dokilly-do.
jokes.setup: Ned's overly friendly greeting with pipe smoking reference
jokes.punchline: Homer's rude dismissal followed by Ned's cheerful nonsense word acceptance
jokes.explanation: Classic Ned characterization with his made-up words and inability to take offense. Great character contrast.

jokes.text: Did you notice how slanted he looked? All part of God's great plan.
jokes.setup: Observation about Ned looking slanted
jokes.punchline: Ned attributing house foundation problems to divine will
jokes.explanation: Ned's religious rationalization of obvious structural problems is perfectly in character and absurd.

jokes.text: 'The Half-Assed Approach to Foundation Repair'
jokes.setup: Homer looking at instructional materials for house repair
jokes.punchline: The manual title perfectly describes Homer's approach to everything
jokes.explanation: Brilliant title that perfectly encapsulates Homer's character while serving the plot. High craft in the wordplay.

jokes.text: Hello, I'm Troy McClure. You might remember me from such instructional videos as Mothballing Your Battleship and Dig Your Own Grave and Save.
jokes.setup: Troy's standard introduction format
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific and morbid instructional video titles
jokes.explanation: Classic Troy McClure format with particularly absurd titles. The 'Dig Your Own Grave' title is darkly brilliant.

jokes.text: Do you have extruded polyvinyl foam insulation? No. Good.
jokes.setup: Technical instruction asking about specific materials
jokes.punchline: The instructor being pleased that Homer doesn't have the right materials
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection - we expect the instructor to be helpful but he's actually pleased by incompetence.

jokes.text: Wait a minute! Now parge the lath. Ohh!
jokes.setup: Rapid-fire technical instructions overwhelming Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer's complete breakdown at meaningless construction jargon
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Homer's frustration with technical incompetence, leading to breakdown.

jokes.text: Let's see. 'Foundation Repair'. Ah, here we are-- D'oh!
jokes.setup: Homer looking up foundation repair in phone book
jokes.punchline: Presumably finding only one listing or bad news, triggering his catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Standard D'oh usage but fits the situation well. Good callback integration.

jokes.text: This is one story that's not on the level!
jokes.setup: News report about the Simpson house foundation problems
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'story' and 'level' for a house foundation issue
jokes.explanation: Decent pun but fairly obvious. Works for the situation but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Meet Cue Ball, the man with no hair! Huh? He's hideous!
jokes.setup: News reporter introducing Homer as a freak show attraction
jokes.punchline: Homer's confused reaction followed by crowd's horror at his baldness
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy playing on Homer's baldness and his obliviousness to being a spectacle.

jokes.text: We only have 500 in the bank, and that leaves... eighty-hundred we need.
jokes.setup: Homer calculating how much money they need for repairs
jokes.punchline: Homer's bizarre math creating the nonsensical term 'eighty-hundred'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer stupidity - creating a nonsensical number that technically works but sounds absurd.

jokes.text: Then I could follow my dream-- living in the woods and keeping a journal of my thoughts.
jokes.setup: Homer's response to Marge getting a job
jokes.punchline: His completely unrealistic and out-of-character 'dream' of philosophical isolation
jokes.explanation: Great subversion - Homer having a 'deep' dream that's completely at odds with his character.

jokes.text: March 15th. I wish I brought a TV. Oh, God, how I miss TV.
jokes.setup: Homer imagining his philosophical journal in the woods
jokes.punchline: His first journal entry immediately revealing his shallow priorities
jokes.explanation: Brilliant immediate payoff to the previous joke - Homer's 'deep thoughts' are just missing TV.

jokes.text: It was the most beautiful, most expensive train ever built-- the Spruce Caboose. Some people said it was too big to stay on the tracks. They were right.
jokes.setup: Dramatic description of a magnificent train
jokes.punchline: Casual admission that the skeptics were right and it crashed
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery about catastrophic failure. The understatement makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Boring!
jokes.setup: Mr. Burns giving a retirement speech
jokes.punchline: Bart's blunt interruption of the formal proceedings
jokes.explanation: Typical Bart disruption. Simple but effective for his character.

jokes.text: Mr. Jack Marley. - Boring!
jokes.setup: Burns continues his speech
jokes.punchline: Same heckler continues interrupting
jokes.explanation: Running gag establishment - repetition creates pattern but lacks sophistication

jokes.text: Oh, 'Me, me, me. I need all the attention just because it's my party,' eh?
jokes.setup: Retiring employee wanting to say a few words at his own party
jokes.punchline: Burns sarcastically mocking the retiree for wanting attention at his own retirement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns selfishness - mocking someone for wanting attention at their own retirement party.

jokes.text: Please don't make me retire. My job is the only thing that keeps me alive. I never married, and my dog is dead.
jokes.setup: Employee pleading not to be retired
jokes.punchline: Escalating depression from job dependency to dead dog
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about workplace depression. Good escalation but fairly predictable.

jokes.text: Forget it, Marge. We already live together. We shouldn't work together. As the Bible says... 'Thou shalt not horn in on thy husband's... racket.'
jokes.setup: Homer invoking biblical authority against Marge working
jokes.punchline: Making up a biblical quote using modern slang
jokes.explanation: Good Homer logic - making up scripture to support his position, using 'racket' anachronistically.

jokes.text: Where does the Bible say 'racket'?
jokes.setup: Homer's fake biblical quote
jokes.punchline: Marge immediately catching the anachronistic language
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge response - catching Homer's obvious fabrication with precision.

jokes.text: Chauffeur. Seamstress. Curator of large mammals?
jokes.setup: Lisa reading Marge's padded resume
jokes.punchline: The jobs getting increasingly absurd, ending with curator of large mammals
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from mundane to absurd. 'Curator of large mammals' is particularly funny.

jokes.text: Lisa, shh! Someone might be listening.
jokes.setup: Lisa mentions Marge voted for Carter twice
jokes.punchline: Marge's paranoid whisper about political affiliation
jokes.explanation: Marge's paranoia about admitting past political choices - character-consistent overcaution

jokes.text: 'Worked for the Carter Administration'? Well, you voted for him... twice!
jokes.setup: Lisa questioning another resume padding
jokes.punchline: Marge's logic that voting equals working for the administration
jokes.explanation: Good Marge logic - her innocent interpretation of what constitutes 'working for' someone.

jokes.text: Let me be the first to say-- [Speaking Foreign Language] What? Welcome aboard. I guess my Swahili's not as good as yours.
jokes.setup: Burns speaking what he thinks is Swahili to impress Marge
jokes.punchline: Marge not understanding and Burns awkwardly covering
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy with Burns trying to show off and failing awkwardly.

jokes.text: And that's good! But here's the bad part. Uh-- Uh-- Ooh! Look at that headline! 'Canada To Hold Referendum.' Sorry, Marge. Can't talk now.
jokes.setup: Homer starting to explain why working together is bad
jokes.punchline: Getting distracted by a completely mundane headline to avoid the conversation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer avoidance behavior - using the most boring possible distraction to escape conversation.

jokes.text: Last week some Jehovah's Witnesses came to the door and I wouldn't let them leave. They snuck away when I went in the kitchen to get more lemonade.
jokes.setup: Marge explaining how boring her life is
jokes.punchline: Reversing the typical Jehovah's Witness scenario - she trapped them instead
jokes.explanation: Brilliant role reversal comedy. Perfect for Marge's character - lonely enough to trap religious visitors.

jokes.text: You'll be just like Marie and Pierre Curie. What did they do? They discovered radium. Then they both died of radiation poisoning.
jokes.setup: Lisa making an encouraging historical comparison
jokes.punchline: The dark reality of what happened to the Curies
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor from Lisa - her intellectual reference backfiring with morbid historical reality.

jokes.text: Cool.
jokes.setup: Discussion of radiation poisoning death
jokes.punchline: Bart's casual approval of the morbid outcome
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart reaction - finding the morbid outcome appealing. Great economy in delivery.

jokes.text: Now, Marge, just remember-- If something goes wrong at the plant, blame the guy who can't speak English. Ah, Tibor, how many times Have you saved my butt?
jokes.setup: Homer giving Marge workplace advice
jokes.punchline: Immediately demonstrating this by thanking Tibor for being his scapegoat
jokes.explanation: Good dark workplace comedy showing Homer's casual exploitation with immediate example.

jokes.text: Do you have dropsy? The grippe? Scrofula? The vapors? Jungle rot? Dandy fever? Poor man's gout? Housemaid's knee? Climatic poopow? The staggers? Dum-dum fever?
jokes.setup: Grampa diagnosing Bart's illness
jokes.punchline: Rapid-fire list of increasingly absurd old-timey diseases
jokes.explanation: Great escalating list with increasingly ridiculous disease names. 'Dum-dum fever' is particularly inspired.

jokes.text: According to your résumé, you invented this machine.
jokes.setup: Marge asking what to do with the machine
jokes.punchline: Lenny pointing out her resume claims she invented it
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the resume padding joke, creating immediate workplace consequences.

jokes.text: Bart, have you ever read 'The Boy Who Cried Wolf'? I'm halfway through it! I swear!
jokes.setup: Teacher asking about the famous fable
jokes.punchline: Bart claiming to be halfway through a very short children's story
jokes.explanation: Good Bart lie - claiming progress on a story that takes minutes to read shows his academic dishonesty.

jokes.text: Marge, Marge, Marge... let's file this under 'R'... for arrivederci.
jokes.setup: Homer helping Marge with filing work
jokes.punchline: Using Italian goodbye as a filing category while throwing it away
jokes.explanation: Good Homer logic - creating a fake filing system for disposal while showing off limited Italian.

jokes.text: Don't worry, baby. The tube'll know what to do.
jokes.setup: Marge unsure where document should go
jokes.punchline: Homer's faith in pneumatic tube's intelligence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - anthropomorphizing inanimate office equipment with complete confidence

jokes.text: Good news, boy. I found a pharmacy that carries leeches! Well, it wasn't exactly a pharmacy. More of a bait shop.
jokes.setup: Grampa announcing good news about medical treatment
jokes.punchline: Revealing he got medical leeches from a fishing bait shop
jokes.explanation: Good Grampa medical ignorance - not distinguishing between medical and fishing leeches.

jokes.text: Oral thermometer, my eye! Think warm thoughts, boy, 'cause this is mighty cold.
jokes.setup: Grampa dismissing modern thermometer methods
jokes.punchline: Implying he's using a rectal thermometer on Bart
jokes.explanation: Typical old-fashioned medical discomfort humor. Works for Grampa's character.

jokes.text: Enchantress! That woman-- She's so captivating. Smithers, my heart's pounding like a jackhammer.
jokes.setup: Burns watching Marge work
jokes.punchline: Burns using industrial metaphor for romantic feelings
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns characterization - describing romance in mechanical terms fits his industrial mindset.

jokes.text: Closer. Closer. Closer, damnit! Ow! Too close.
jokes.setup: Burns demanding Smithers zoom the camera closer to see Marge
jokes.punchline: Going too far and presumably getting hurt by the camera
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy with good escalation and payoff.

jokes.text: I am the angel of death. The time of purification is at hand. Oh, who cares.
jokes.setup: Carl making dramatic apocalyptic pronouncement
jokes.punchline: Immediately giving up with casual indifference
jokes.explanation: Excellent deflation comedy - going from biblical drama to workplace apathy instantly.

jokes.text: You mean like Child Labor Day?
jokes.setup: Marge suggesting theme days to boost morale
jokes.punchline: Burns immediately suggesting an exploitative theme day
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns evil - hearing 'theme days' and immediately thinking of exploiting children.

jokes.text: You can't take our doughnuts! Right. Anyone else want to be a hero?
jokes.setup: Worker protesting healthy snack replacement
jokes.punchline: Immediate capitulation when challenged, warning others not to resist
jokes.explanation: Good workplace cowardice comedy - immediate surrender after brave protest.

jokes.text: My ovaries!
jokes.setup: Bart needing another excuse to avoid his test
jokes.punchline: Male character claiming female anatomy problems
jokes.explanation: Good Bart desperation - grabbing any medical excuse without considering biological impossibility.

jokes.text: Boy cries 'wolf,' has a few laughs. I forget how it ends.
jokes.setup: Bart claiming knowledge of 'The Boy Who Cried Wolf'
jokes.punchline: Completely missing the moral point of the famous story
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart - completely missing the moral lesson and focusing on the 'fun' parts.

jokes.text: Are you kidding? I work like a Japanese beaver.
jokes.setup: Homer defending his work ethic
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical animal comparison combining nationality and species randomly
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer logic - combining 'busy as a beaver' with Japanese work ethic stereotypes nonsensically.

jokes.text: Well, I won't sleep in the same bed with a woman who thinks I'm lazy. I'm gonna go right downstairs, unfold the couch, unroll the sleeping ba-- Eh, good night.
jokes.setup: Homer dramatically declaring he'll sleep elsewhere in protest
jokes.punchline: Getting tired just from describing the effort required and giving up
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer laziness - getting exhausted just from describing the minimal effort required for his protest.

jokes.text: You know that dream where you're in bed, and they fly in through the window? [Moaning]
jokes.setup: Burns describing dreams about Marge
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate sexual fantasy described to employee
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable workplace harassment - Burns oversharing his fantasies with Smithers

jokes.text: Oh, that's right. He's dead. And rather pungent.
jokes.setup: Burns remembering they dug up Al Jolson before
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning the corpse's smell as an afterthought
jokes.explanation: Good Burns casual evil - treating corpse desecration as a minor inconvenience.

jokes.text: These spurs are killing me. Ey-y-y. Sit on it. Lenny, '50s day is next Wednesday.
jokes.setup: Carl apparently dressed inappropriately for the current theme day
jokes.punchline: Being told he's got the wrong decade and needs to change
jokes.explanation: Decent callback to theme days with period costume confusion.

jokes.text: I've got some washers in my truck. I'll give you one. Marge, get my gun.
jokes.setup: Repairman offering a simple 50-cent solution
jokes.punchline: Homer's violent overreaction to helpful customer service
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - Homer's paranoid reaction to genuine helpfulness shows his character perfectly.

jokes.text: Well, I'm not sure I'm over it. Shove it, witch. [Barking]
jokes.setup: Bart claiming to still have rabies
jokes.punchline: Demonstrating with aggressive barking and insults
jokes.explanation: Good Bart excuse-making with physical comedy demonstration.

jokes.text: Tourette's syndrome. [Barking]
jokes.setup: Bart continuing to make medical excuses
jokes.punchline: Claiming Tourette's while making dog sounds
jokes.explanation: Darker humor using medical condition as excuse, less clever than other Bart moments.

jokes.text: That's our secret word for the day!
jokes.setup: Someone mentions 'loud' near the wolf exhibit
jokes.punchline: Triggering alarms because it was the secret word on a game show
jokes.explanation: Good escalation setup leading to chaos. Nice misdirection about why the alarms go off.

jokes.text: She must think you're after her eggs. I only ate one!
jokes.setup: Explanation for why the bird is attacking Bart
jokes.punchline: Bart's confession revealing he actually did steal an egg
jokes.explanation: Good Bart moment - his defensive confession reveals he actually did the thing he's being accused of.

jokes.text: 'Ivanhoe is the story of a Russian farmer and his tool.'
jokes.setup: Bart taking his test while being attacked
jokes.punchline: Completely wrong summary of the classic novel
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart academic failure - completely misunderstanding classic literature in absurd way.

jokes.text: You... have a husband? Yes. I can picture him now-- rugged good looks, sweater knotted about his shoulders... curly locks shining in the sun like spun gold!
jokes.setup: Burns asking about Marge's husband
jokes.punchline: Marge's romantic description being the complete opposite of Homer
jokes.explanation: Great irony - Marge's romantic idealization being completely opposite to actual Homer.

jokes.text: The senile old fool.
jokes.setup: Marge mentioning Mr. Burns gave her another raise
jokes.punchline: Homer calling the man enriching them a fool
jokes.explanation: Good Homer obliviousness - insulting the person who's benefiting him.

jokes.text: You don't have to sue me to get my pants off. [Purrs]
jokes.setup: Marge threatening to sue Burns
jokes.punchline: Burns making an inappropriate sexual advance
jokes.explanation: Burns sexual harassment played for cringe comedy. Fits character but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Hey, wolfie, putdown that hors d'oeuvre. It's time for the main course!
jokes.setup: Willie addressing the wolf that's attacking people
jokes.punchline: Treating the human victim as an appetizer and threatening worse
jokes.explanation: Good Willie moment - treating the dangerous situation with his typical aggressive confidence.

jokes.text: Hey, wolfie, put down that hors d'oeuvre. It's time for the main course!
jokes.setup: Wolf attacking Bart
jokes.punchline: Willie treating student as appetizer, himself as main course
jokes.explanation: Perfect Willie characterization - his Scottish toughness makes him eager to fight the wolf

jokes.text: There was no wolf. I faked the whole thing. I'm just gonna lie on the floor now. Please don't let me swallow my tongue.
jokes.setup: Bart finally telling the truth about the wolf
jokes.punchline: Immediately having a breakdown and asking for medical help
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the Boy Who Cried Wolf theme with Bart's complete breakdown from guilt.

jokes.text: I was wrestling wolves back when you were at your mother's teat.
jokes.setup: Willie boasting about his wolf-fighting experience
jokes.punchline: Using crude imagery to emphasize how long he's been doing it
jokes.explanation: Classic Willie crude boasting with his typical colorful language.

jokes.text: Care to join me in a belt of scotch? It's 9:30 in the morning. Yeah, but I haven't slept in days.
jokes.setup: Lawyer offering alcohol early in the morning
jokes.punchline: His justification that sleeplessness makes it acceptable
jokes.explanation: Standard bad lawyer trope humor. Functional but not particularly original.

jokes.text: Hey! It's full of shredded newspapers.
jokes.setup: Expensive lawyer leaving his briefcase behind
jokes.punchline: It containing worthless shredded paper instead of legal documents
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag about legal profession fakery. Simple but effective.

jokes.text: Gotcha. Marge, we're gettin' some drive-through; then we're doing it twice.
jokes.setup: Burns telling Homer to show Marge the time of her life
jokes.punchline: Homer's crude interpretation of romance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer romanticism - combining fast food with crude sexual reference as his idea of special.

jokes.text: Get help, luv. Call Interpol, get me a hacksaw, anything.
jokes.setup: Tom Jones being forced to perform at Burns' party
jokes.punchline: Desperately asking for international police help to escape
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of desperation. The Interpol reference adds absurdist element.
